name: Design lead strategic multi-user input — tests complexity and scale handling

input.userMessage: TeamPulse serves 3 user types: (1) ICs in feed/wiki daily — need fast creation and discovery. (2) Team leads need at-a-glance health — who's active, blocked, what decisions made. (3) Org admins manage infrastructure — SSO, billing, compliance. Each has different home screen. WCAG AA, 15+ languages (RTL), teams from 5 to 5000. Series B — consistent patterns > pixel-perfect.

expectedBehaviors: Captures all 3 user types with distinct goals and frustrations
Researches both linear.app and notion.so for density and clarity patterns
accessibilityContext includes RTL and localization requirements
product.json reflects scale range (5 to 5000 members)
designDecisions includes 'consistent patterns over pixel-perfect' philosophy
layoutConventions differentiates home screen by user type
Figma analysis still runs to find existing patterns
antiPatterns: Flattens 3 user types into a single generic 'user' persona
Does not research both URLs when both were provided
Misses WCAG AA or RTL requirements in output
Does not differentiate home screen experiences by user type
Ignores the 'consistent patterns > pixel-perfect' design philosophy

evaluationCriteria.userTypeCapture: Are all 3 user types preserved with distinct goals, not flattened?
evaluationCriteria.accessibilityCapture: Are WCAG AA and RTL/localization in the output?
evaluationCriteria.scaleAwareness: Does the output reflect the 5-to-5000 team size range?
evaluationCriteria.dualUrlResearch: Were both linear.app and notion.so researched?
